title: trifolium subterraneum subsp. oxaloides
intro: Trifolium subterraneum subsp. oxaloides, also known as the subterranean clover oxaloides, is a perennial legume native to Australia, characterized by its small, pink or white flowers and trifoliate leaves. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include being a natural diuretic and antiseptic, making it effective in treating kidney stones and urinary tract infections. The plant is also known to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving digestion. Trifolium subterraneum subsp. oxaloides can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine to support overall health and well-being.
